findings.message: plugin.json has no explicit skills array; relies on auto-discovery of skills/ subdirectory, which is fragile if plugin loader behaviour changes
MCP server runs chrome-devtools-mcp@latest via npx; version floats on every MCP client restart introducing supply-chain risk
prepare script executes scripts/prepare.ts automatically on npm install and npm publish without explicit user invocation
Most devDependencies use ^ semver ranges allowing automatic minor and patch updates on each npm install
"standard a11y queries" uses imprecise adjective; reader cannot determine which tool calls or checks qualify as standard
"they are extremely large" uses imprecise intensifier with no concrete size bound to guide the model
"standard a11y queries" uses imprecise adjective; reader cannot determine which tool calls or checks qualify as standard
"they are extremely large" uses imprecise intensifier with no concrete size bound to guide the model
plugin.json has no explicit skills array; relies on auto-discovery of skills/ subdirectory, which is fragile if plugin loader behaviour changes

prepare script executes scripts/prepare.ts automatically on npm install and npm publish without explicit user invocation — "prepare": "node --experimental-strip-types scripts/prepare.ts" confirmed at line 27; npm runs prepare automatically on install and publish

MCP server runs chrome-devtools-mcp@latest via npx; version floats on every MCP client restart introducing supply-chain risk — "args": ["chrome-devtools-mcp@latest"] confirmed at line 9 of .claude-plugin/plugin.json

Most devDependencies use ^ semver ranges allowing automatic minor and patch updates on each npm install — devDependencies block (lines 49-83) contains entries like "@eslint/js": "^9.35.0" using ^ range operator throughout
